Re: Substitute Bill No. 28-38 (COR), "AN ACT TO AMEND 28112(c) OF CHAPTER
28, TITLE 11, GUAM CODE ANNOTATED, RELATIVE TO INCREASING
GUAM ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY'S COMPLIANCE AND
ENFORCEMENT CAPACITY TO PROTECT THE HEALTH OF OUR
ENVIRONMENT AND PEOPLE."
Bill No. 83-38 (COR), "AN ACT TOAMEND 1203.1 OF ARTICLE 2, CHAPTER
1, TITLE 5, GUAM CODE ANNOTATED; AND 45112 OF ARTICLE 1,
CHAPTER 45, TITLE 10, GUAM CODE ANNOTATED, RELATIVE TO
ALLOWING THE BUREAU OF STATISTICS AND PLANS GUAM COASTAL
MANAGEMENT PROGRAM AND THE GUAM ENVIRONMENTAL
PROTECTION AGENCY TO EMPLOY ENVIRONMENTAL LAW ATTORNEYS
FOR THEIR EXCLUSIVE USE."
f-låfaAdai Mr. Speaker,
The preservation of our natural environment is ofvital importance to our community. Guam is
the homeland ofthe CHamoru people, who have acted as stewards ofthe islandfor thousands of
years, and whose culture and very survival depend on the responsible use and protection ofour
natural environment and resources through conservation and sustainable practices. The health of
our island's economy is also tied to responsible care and management of our natural
environment, to ensure the continued productivity ofour island. To this end, preservation ofour
island has always required careful balancingofhuman needs with environmental protection.
Our government's environmental policy, and applicableU.S. environmental policy,is carried out
by several agencies including the Guam Environmental Protection Agency ("GEPA") and the
Guam Coastal Management Program ("GCMP")ofthe Bureau ofStatistics and Plans ("BSP").
This session, the Legislature passed two bills intended to bolster capacity within these agencies
to address the increasing demands they face in their efforts to execute their respective
responsibilities.These bills are addressed in turn herein.
RICARDOJ.BORDALLOGOVERNOR'SCOMPLEX
513W.MarineCorpsDrive Hagåtna, Guam969IO
governor.guam.gov | (671) 472-8931
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0739.*

To: The Hon. FrankBlas, Jr.,Speaker Page2 of3
Fr: TheHon. LourdesA. Leon Guerrero,Governor ofGuam
Date: June 17,2025
SubstituteBill No. 28-38 (COR) and 83-38 (COR)
SubstituteBillNo. 28-38
Substitute Bill No. 28-38 (COR) proposes to provide a continuing appropriation to the GEPA
Environmental Trust Fund, which would enable the agency to increase capacity to perform the
agency's regulatoryfunctions.
The GEPA was established to provide a united, integrated and comprehensive program for
environmental protection, to safeguard our island from degradation in quality ofour land, water
and air. Its powers and responsibilities were numerous and varied when it was first established in
1972, but have vastly expanded in the decades since. And like other regulatory agencies in
Guam, the GEPA faces significant challenges in recruitment and retention ofqualified staff.
Guam law requires persons importing property into Guam to pay a tax for the use of such
property at a rate measured by the landed value ofthe property, also known as a "use tax." Title
II ofthe Guam Code Annotated provides that 20% ofthe total use tax collected in the previous
fiscal year shall be appropriated to the GEPA Environmental Cleanup Fund to cover costs
associated with preserving Guam's natural resources, continued on the annual submission ofa
detailed budget to the Legislature. Bill 28 would shift the allocation of these funds from the
cleanup fund to the Guam Environmental Trust Fund, which would enable GEPA to hire key
employees to address its ongoing critical staffing shortage.
While I support Bill 28's intent, unfortunately, I must veto the bill dueto its fiscal impact on the
current fiscal year's budget. Section 3 of the bill provides that the act shall be effective upon
enactment. However, those portions ofthe use tax allocated to the cleanup fund are not presently
available to be diverted to the trust fund. This is because the appropriation reflected in the
current law, while continuous, is contingent on GEPA's annual submission ofa detailedbudget to
the Legislature. GEPA did not submit the budget to the Legislature to trigger appropriation of
Use Tax funds to the cleanup fund for FY25, and those funds that would otherwisebe available
have been appropriated by the Legislature for other purposes. The soonest Bill 28 can be
implemented is FY26.
For this reason, Bill No. 28-38 is VETOED. I urge the bill's sponsorto reintroduce this bill for
prospective implementation.
BillNo. 83-38
Bill No. 83-38 is intended to address a specific, pervasive need within our environmental
agencies legal services. Because federal and local statutory and regulatory schemes governing
—
environmental issues are constantly evolving, our regulatory agencies would greatly benefit from
embedded legal counsel who can, overtime, develop the necessary expertise to effectivelyadvise
these agencies. For this reason, prior authorizations allowing agencies to hire contract attorneys
did not sufficiently meet agency needs. Bill 83 authorizes GEPA and GCMP to hire inhouse
attorneys within the classified service. While GCMP requested authorization to hire LTA
attorneys as an interim measure while theyrequest funding and authorizationfrom their grantors,
which was not ultimatelyadopted by the Legislature, codification ofBill 83 will certainly benefit
GCMP in the future.
RICARDOJ. BORDALLOGOVERNOR'S COMPLEX
513W. MarineCorpsDrive Hagåtha, Guam 969IO
governor.guam.gov | (671) 472-8931
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0739.*

To: The Hon. Frank Blas, Jr.,Speaker Page3 of3
Fr: The Hon. LourdesA. LeonGuerrero, Governor ofGuam
Date: June 17,2025
Substitute Bill No. 28-38 (COR) and 83-38 (COR)
For this reason, I am vetoing Substitute BillNo. 28-38 (COR) and signing BillNo. 83-38 (COR)
into law as PublicLawNo. 38-15.
Senseramente,
JJ-LA
LOURDESA. LEON GUERRERO
IMaga'hågan Guåhan
Governor of Guam

AND THE GUAM ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY TO

AN ACT TO AMEND 1203.1 OF ARTICLE 2, CHAPTER
1, TITLE 5, GUAM CODE ANNOTATED;AND 45112 OF
ARTICLE 1, CHAPTER 45, TITLE 10, GUAM CODE
ANNOTATED, RELATIVE TO ALLOWING THE
BUREAU OF STATISTICS AND PLANS GUAM
COASTAL MANAGEMENT PROGRAM AND THE
GUAM ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY TO
EMPLOY ENVIRONMENTAL LAW ATTORNEYS FOR
THEIR EXCLUSIVE USE.
1 BE IT ENACTED BY THE PEOPLE OF GUAM:
2 Section 1. Legislative Findings and Intent. I Liheslaturan Guåhan finds
3 that the enactment of Public Law 37-130 did not solve the needs of the Guam
1
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0739.*

I Environmental Protection Agency (GEPA) and the Guam Coastal Management
2 Program (GCMP) to employ full-time attorneys for legal services. Both these entities
3 are federally funded and grantorfederal agenciesboth encourage use offederal funds
4 for the hiring of a full-time attorney.
5 I Liheslaturan Guåhan further finds that the main issues, in the past, was
6 employment ofattorneys with eitherlocal or federal funds but diverting the attorneys
7 to provide legal services for agencies and programs that are unrelatedto GEPA's and
8 GCMP's missions.
9 ILiheslaturan Guåhan further finds Public Law 37-130requires the obtain of
IO legal services through the procurementprocess only and does not allow employment
11 of classified employees. The enactment of Public Law 28-187 was intended
12 specifically for federally funded employees. It allowed employees to be hired
13 competitively though the merit system, provided grievance and adverse action
14 appeals rights, protection from political activity but allowed for termination in the
15 event that federal funds ceased to be available to fund their employment.
16 The proscription on the use of classified employees for legal services is
17 inconsistent with the provisions of 48 U.S.C. 1422c(a) and the expectations of
18 federal grantors for personnel staffing of state and local grantee agencies.
19 It is the intent ofILiheslaturan Guåhan to amend the sections oflaw enacted
20 by Public Law 37-130 to allow the GEPA and the GCMP to each employ a staff
21 attorney.
22 Section 2. 1203.1 ofArticle 2, Chapter l, Title 5, Guam CodeAnnotated,
23 is amended to read as follows:
24 "S 1203.1. Environmental Law Attorney for the Bureau of
25 Statistics and Plans Coastal Management Program.
26 Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Guam Coastal
27 Management Program of the Bureau of Statistics and Plans is authorized to
2
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0739.*

1 use its federal funds to hire an attorney for its exclusive use. The attorney shall
2 be hired as a classified employee pursuant to the provisions of 4103(g) and
3 4103 (h) of Chapter 4, Title 4 Guam Code Annotated or to procure the
4 services of an attorney pursuant to and in accordance with the Guam
5 Procurement process for professional services."
6 Section 3. 45112 of Article l, Chapter 45, Title IO, Guam Code
7 Annotated, is amendedto read as follows:
8 "S 45112. Environmental Law Attorney for the Guam
9 Environmental Protection Agency.
10 Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Guam Environmental
11 ProtectionAgency is authorized to use its federal funds to hire an attorney for
12 its exclusive use. The attorney shall be hired as a classifiedemployee pursuant
13 to the provisions of 4103(g) and 4103 (h) ofChapter 4, Title 4 Guam Code
14 Annotated or to procure the services of an attorney pursuant to and in
15 accordance with the Guam Procurement process for professional services."
